questions.q: What does the word 'Hanukkah' mean in Hebrew?
How many nights does the Hanukkah holiday last?
How many candles are in a traditional Hanukkah menorah?
What is the name of the center candle on a Hanukkah menorah?
According to the Hanukkah story, for how many nights did the oil miraculously burn in the Temple?
Which ancient group did the Maccabees revolt against during the Hanukkah story?
In which country is Hanukkah observed as a national holiday?
What is Hanukkah commonly called?
What type of oil is traditionally used to light the candles in a Hanukkah menorah?
Why is it customary to fry foods during Hanukkah?
choices: To symbolize the miracle of the oil burning for eight nights

questions.q: What is the significance of the Western Wall in relation to Hanukkah?
choices: It marks the starting point of the Maccabean revolt
It is where the first menorah was lit
It is the last remnant of the Temple
It is where oil was stored during ancient times

questions.q: During which season of the year does Hanukkah typically occur?
What is the most well-known tradition associated with Hanukkah?
On which night of Hanukkah is the most number of candles lit?
What prize can be won by playing the dreidel game?
In modern Israel, from which city does an annual torch relay begin for Hanukkah?
What is the primary reason Hanukkah is celebrated as a festival of lights?
choices: To mark the beginning of spring
To celebrate the return of light during the darkest time of year
To commemorate the invention of candles

questions.q: What historical event does Hanukkah commemorate?
choices: The building of the Second Temple
The giving of the Torah at Mount Sinai
The Maccabean revolt against Syrian-Greek forces

questions.q: What is the purpose of the center candle on a Hanukkah menorah?
choices: To be used to light all the other candles
To represent the eighth day of the holiday

questions.q: In which European country did the dreidel game originate before being adopted by Jewish communities?
Where is the menorah traditionally displayed in homes outside of Israel during Hanukkah?
What was the original material traditionally used to make a dreidel?
What is the destination of the Hanukkah torch relay in modern-day Israel?
What is the primary reason the menorah is commonly displayed in a window during Hanukkah celebrations outside of Israel?
choices: To keep it away from children
To protect it from wind and rain
To make the light visible to passersby and share the message of the holiday
To signal other Jewish families in the neighborhood

questions.q: Which ancient historian, writing in the first century CE, provided one of the earliest written accounts of the Hanukkah story and the Maccabean revolt?
According to Jewish law, at what minimum height should a Hanukkah menorah be positioned above ground level when placed in a doorway?
In the Books of Maccabees, what was the original dedication festival that Hanukkah was modeled after in terms of its eight-day duration?
What is the Hebrew term for the rededication of the Second Temple by Judah Maccabee, from which the holiday's name derives?
Which Syrian-Greek ruler's desecration of the Second Temple by installing a statue of Zeus precipitated the Maccabean revolt?
According to the Talmud in Shabbat 21b, what specific olive oil was originally used to fuel the menorah in the rededicated temple?
choices: Oil pressed three days before the festival
Oil from the Mount of Olives
Oil that was sealed with the High Priest's seal

questions.q: In traditional Ashkenazi custom, on which night of Hanukkah is it customary to give gelt (money) to children?
What mathematical progression describes the number of candles lit each night of Hanukkah according to the House of Hillel's tradition?
Which four Hebrew letters appear on the sides of a traditional dreidel, and what do they collectively abbreviate?
choices: Shin, Mem, Nun, Yud—the name of God
Tau, Shin, Resh, Qof—'turn and turn again'
Aleph, Bet, Gimel, Dalet—the first four letters of the alphabet
Nun, Gimel, He, Shin—'A great miracle happened there'

questions.q: In medieval Jewish communities, what was the primary reason for frying foods during Hanukkah rather than baking them?
choices: Oil was an available commodity and commemorated the oil miracle
Fried foods lasted longer without refrigeration
Baking was prohibited on holidays in Ashkenazi law
Ovens were community property and unavailable for private use

questions.q: According to the First Book of Maccabees, for how long had the temple altar remained profaned before Judah Maccabee's rededication?
What is the name of the prayer recited after lighting the Hanukkah candles that begins with 'Blessed are You, Lord our God'?
In the Hellenistic period, what Jewish religious practice did Antiochus IV explicitly forbid, making its observance a capital offense?
Which Jewish holiday, if it occurs during the intermediate days of Hanukkah, overrides the regular candle-lighting ceremony?
choices: Shabbat requires postponement until after nightfall
Shabbat does not override; candles are still lit in the home

questions.q: In Jewish tradition, what is the minimum number of witnesses required to establish the proper time for kindling the Hanukkah menorah in the ancient Temple?
What is the specific name of the piyyut (liturgical poem) composed in medieval times that recounts the Hanukkah story and is sung in some communities?
According to the Talmud, if one is unable to light candles personally, what alternative method is permitted?
choices: Another household member may light the candles on their behalf
They must use electric lights instead
They must hire a professional candle-lighter

questions.q: In the Second Century BCE conflict, the Maccabees were primarily opposed to which cultural and religious imposition by the Seleucid authorities?
choices: Forced Hellenization and the prohibition of Jewish religious practice

questions.q: According to Mishnaic tradition, on which side of the doorway was the menorah originally positioned during Hanukkah?
choices: On the left side of the doorway
On both sides of the doorway equally
On the right side of the doorway

questions.q: What was the primary catalyst for the relocation of menorah placement practices from outside the door to windowsills during the diaspora?
choices: An architectural shift in Jewish home construction
A preference for increased interior lighting
A change in rabbinic law regarding public displays
The harsh realities of persecution and diaspora conditions

questions.q: What symbolic purpose does the placement of both the mezuzah and menorah on either side of a doorway serve in Jewish practice?
choices: To surround a person with holiness through the performance of mitzvot
To balance the aesthetic appearance of the entrance
To indicate the entrance to a Jewish household to travelers
To provide double protection against negative spiritual forces

questions.q: During the Hanukkah festival in schools throughout Israel, students traditionally participate in which of the following activities?
In the Talmudic discussion of Hanukkah observance, what is the significance of progressively adding one additional candle each successive night?
choices: It represents the number of days the Maccabees fought in battle
It mirrors the decreasing duration of daylight in winter
It commemorates each of the eight Temple vessels
It symbolizes the increasing brightness of hope and redemption

questions.q: Which rabbinic authority is credited with establishing the custom that the menorah should be lit in a window or doorway rather than strictly indoors?
choices: The consensus of Mishnaic scholars based on diaspora realities

questions.q: What is the name of the four-sided spinning top used in Hanukkah games, and what do its four Hebrew letters represent?
choices: The trendel; the four pillars of Jewish faith
The dreitel; the four nights of initial darkness
The dreidel; the four corners of the Temple
The dreidel; an acronym for 'A great miracle happened there'

questions.q: In the ancient ceremony of the menorah relay in contemporary Israel, who traditionally receives the torch from the previous torchbearer to light the ceremonial giant menorah?
choices: A rotating selection of historically or culturally significant individuals
The youngest child present at the ceremony

questions.q: Which group of Jewish fighters led the rebellion against Syrian-Greek forces during the Hanukkah period?
Why did European Jews adapt the dreidel by adding Hebrew letters to this gambling game?
choices: To make the game more difficult
To increase the monetary value of the game
To prevent gentiles from understanding the game
To engage with Jewish scripture and practice Hebrew

questions.q: Which public building in Israel displays prominent menorahs during the Hanukkah season?
What type of foods are characteristically fried during Hanukkah celebrations?
choices: Foods such as latkes and sufganiyot

questions.q: According to traditional Jewish teaching, what will change regarding the placement of the menorah when the Messiah arrives?
choices: The menorah will no longer be used in observance
The menorah will be moved inside the home permanently
The menorah will be placed outside on the left side of the doorway
The menorah will be displayed only in synagogues

questions.q: What was the desecration of the Holy Temple by the Syrian-Greeks that prompted the Maccabean revolt?
choices: They forced the High Priest into exile
They stole the Temple's treasury and treasures
They completely demolished the Temple structure
They prohibited Jewish religious practices and defiled the Temple

questions.q: Which school of Jewish thought insisted that eight candles be lit on the first night of Hanukkah?
What do the four Hebrew letters on a dreidel spell when combined?
choices: The name of the Maccabean leader

questions.q: During which month of the Hebrew calendar does Hanukkah typically fall?
What miracle does Hanukkah commemorate?
choices: Olive oil burning for 8 nights when only enough for 1
The parting of the Red Sea
Joshua and the walls of Jericho

source.original_a: The oil that burned for eight nights
source.notes: Miracle of the cruse of oil at the rededication of the Second Temple.

questions.q: What spinning top is played with on Hanukkah?
What four Hebrew letters appear on a dreidel?
choices: Nun, Gimel, Hey, Shin (or Pey in Israel)

source.notes: Stand for 'Nes Gadol Hayah Sham' = 'A Great Miracle Happened There' (or 'Po' = 'Here' in Israel).

questions.q: What fried potato pancake is traditionally eaten on Hanukkah?

source.notes: Fried in oil — symbolic of the miracle of the oil.

questions.q: What jelly-filled fried doughnut is the Israeli Hanukkah treat?
What chocolate coins are given as Hanukkah gifts?

source.notes: From Yiddish for 'money.' Used both for gifting and dreidel-game stakes.

questions.q: Approximately what time of year does Hanukkah fall (in the Hebrew calendar month of Kislev)?

source.notes: 25th of Kislev (Hebrew calendar) = somewhere between late Nov and late Dec.

questions.q: Which century BCE did the Maccabean revolt take place?
Which letter on a dreidel means 'put in' (the player adds gelt to the pot)?

source.notes: Nun = nothing; Gimel = take all; Hey = take half; Shin = put one in.

questions.q: Which letter on a dreidel means 'take all' (the spinner wins the pot)?
Which classic children's song begins 'I have a little dreidel, I made it out of clay...'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed incorrect '1958 Bialik-style' framing (song was written 1927; Bialik unrelated). English lyrics by Samuel S. Grossman; music by Samuel E. Goldfarb (1927).

questions.q: Which special blessing is recited only on the first night of Hanukkah (and not on the remaining nights) when lighting the menorah?
